---
title: "Topic Modeling for Good Taste Definitions"
---

::: {.callout-note}
This report was generated using AI under general human direction. At the time of generation, the contents have not been comprehensively reviewed by a human analyst.
:::

# Introduction

This document uses **BERTopic** to extract topics from open-ended survey responses about what constitutes "Good Taste" and "Bad Taste". Since the text is short and survey-based (1-5 sentences), BERTopic's embedding and clustering approach is highly effective. We are using the cleaned dataset (`FolkTaste_CleanData.csv`) which contains numeric encodings and excludes participants who were filtered out during quality checks.

# Setup and data loading

We begin by loading the required packages and reading in the clean data.

```{python}
#| label: load-packages
import pandas as pd
from bertopic import BERTopic
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data
df = pd.read_csv("data/FolkTaste_CleanData.csv")

# The questionnaire skip pattern dictates that only people who answered YES to Taste_Possibility
# provided definitions for GoodTaste_Def and BadTaste_Def.
df_filtered = df[df['Taste_Possibility'] == 'YES'].copy()

# Extract the "GoodTaste_Def" and "BadTaste_Def" columns and drop empty responses
good_taste_docs = df_filtered["GoodTaste_Def"].dropna().astype(str).tolist()
bad_taste_docs = df_filtered["BadTaste_Def"].dropna().astype(str).tolist()
```

The dataset contains `{python} len(good_taste_docs)` valid responses defining "Good Taste" and `{python} len(bad_taste_docs)` responses defining "Bad Taste".

# Good taste topic model

We initialize a `CountVectorizer` to remove common English stop words, then fit the `BERTopic` model to the Good Taste documents.

```{python}
#| label: fit-good-taste
vectorizer_model = CountVectorizer(stop_words="english")

good_topic_model = BERTopic(
    language="english", 
    calculate_probabilities=True, 
    verbose=True,
    vectorizer_model=vectorizer_model
)

good_topics, good_probs = good_topic_model.fit_transform(good_taste_docs)
good_topic_model.get_topic_info().head(10)
```

## Visualizing good taste topics

The interactive Intertopic Distance Map allows us to explore the semantic distance between the generated clusters.

```{python}
#| label: visualize-good-taste-distance
good_topic_model.visualize_topics()
```

To understand the core themes of each topic, we can visualize the most important words that make up the top topics.

```{python}
#| label: visualize-good-taste-barchart
good_topic_model.visualize_barchart(top_n_topics=8)
```

Because short survey responses often share similar language, a hierarchical clustering dendrogram helps us see how sub-themes relate to broader themes of Good Taste.

```{python}
#| label: visualize-good-taste-hierarchy
good_topic_model.visualize_hierarchy()
```

# Bad taste topic model

We repeat the same process for the Bad Taste documents, utilizing the same vectorizer to remove stop words.

```{python}
#| label: fit-bad-taste
bad_topic_model = BERTopic(
    language="english", 
    calculate_probabilities=True, 
    verbose=True,
    vectorizer_model=vectorizer_model
)

bad_topics, bad_probs = bad_topic_model.fit_transform(bad_taste_docs)
bad_topic_model.get_topic_info().head(10)
```

## Visualizing bad taste topics

```{python}
#| label: visualize-bad-taste-distance
bad_topic_model.visualize_topics()
```

```{python}
#| label: visualize-bad-taste-barchart
bad_topic_model.visualize_barchart(top_n_topics=8)
```

```{python}
#| label: visualize-bad-taste-hierarchy
bad_topic_model.visualize_hierarchy()
```

# Export models and summaries

Finally, we export the topics, their main words, and the representative documents to easily readable HTML files. We also save the BERTopic models to disk so they can be loaded later without having to recompute the embeddings.

```{python}
#| label: export-results
# Export summary tables to HTML
good_info = good_topic_model.get_topic_info()
good_info.to_html("good_taste_summary_table.html", index=False)

bad_info = bad_topic_model.get_topic_info()
bad_info.to_html("bad_taste_summary_table.html", index=False)

# Save the models to disk
good_topic_model.save("good_taste_model", serialization="safetensors", save_ctfidf=True)
bad_topic_model.save("bad_taste_model", serialization="safetensors", save_ctfidf=True)
```
